 {"id":"bd-2nkmi","title":"Mermaid Showcase Super Screen (over-the-top interactive demo)","description":"Purpose
- Build a new, over-the-top interactive demo screen that makes the Mermaid pipeline visible and impressive: parse -> IR -> layout -> render, with live controls and performance instrumentation.
- Demonstrate fast, deterministic recomputation of "optimal" layouts across many complex samples.

User Value / Why
- Turns the existing mermaid capabilities in ftui-extras into a flagship, testable, keyboard-first demo.
- Gives clear evidence of correctness, determinism, and performance budgets.

Scope
- New screen in ftui-demo-showcase with rich UI (header, sample list, viewport, controls, metrics, status log).
- Large curated sample library spanning multiple Mermaid diagram types and edge cases.
- Interactive controls for layout/fidelity/viewport (including viewport size overrides) and a metrics panel showing timing + quality.

Non-Goals
- No full Mermaid editor; samples are read-only.
- No backward compat shims; update core code directly if needed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Screen renders complex Mermaid examples deterministically for fixed inputs.
- Layout/fidelity/viewport changes visibly update output and metrics.
- Metrics panel shows parse/layout/render times and layout quality fields.
- Works at 80x24 (minimal), 120x40 (default), 200x60 (max showcase).
- Unit tests, snapshots, and e2e harness runs validate behavior with detailed logs.

Testing Requirements (must be included by child tasks)
- Unit tests: state transitions, keybindings, rendering triggers, error handling.
- Snapshot tests: 3+ sizes x 3+ diagram types, with metrics visibility + viewport size overrides.
- E2E scripts: deterministic seed, exit-after, JSONL log validation, clear failure messages.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.1","title":"Spec the Mermaid showcase screen UX + interaction model","description":"What (spec already drafted in docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md)
- This bead captures the UX + interaction model and acts as source of truth for implementation.

Key UX decisions (summary, keep in-sync with spec file)
- Screen regions: header (title, sample index, layout, fidelity, status), sample list, render viewport, control panel, metrics panel, footer hints.
- Layout rules by size:
  - 80x24: collapsed sample selector, compact metrics line, control overlay toggle.
  - 120x40: 3-column layout (sample list | viewport | controls+metrics).
  - 200x60: expanded layout with optional status log panel.
- Keybindings (required set):
  - Sample nav: Up/Down or j/k, Home/End, Enter (re-render)
  - Zoom: +/-, 0 reset, f fit
  - Viewport size override: [ / ] adjust width, { / } adjust height (or equivalent explicit mapping)
  - Layout: l cycle mode (Auto/Dense/Spacious), r force re-layout
  - Panels: m toggle metrics, c toggle controls
  - Fidelity: t cycle tier, g glyph mode, s styles, w wrap mode
  - Help: ? overlay, Esc close overlay
- State model: selected_index, layout_mode, fidelity, glyph_mode, wrap_mode, styles_enabled, metrics_visible, controls_visible,
  viewport_zoom/pan, viewport_size_override (w,h), last_render report, last_error.
- Metrics fields: parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms, layout_iterations, objective_score, constraint_violations, fallback_tier, fallback_reason.

Acceptance
- Spec is self-contained and includes the above details; no external plan needed.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.10","title":"E2E harness coverage for Mermaid showcase","description":"What
- Add e2e harness coverage for the Mermaid screen.

E2E requirements
- Run screen with fixed seed and exit-after.
- Capture structured logs and validate JSONL schema + output hash.
- Clear failure messages that include sample, mode, and timings.

Acceptance
- CI can run the new e2e target deterministically with actionable logs.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.2","title":"Create Mermaid sample library (comprehensive + extreme cases)","description":"What
- Build a curated Mermaid sample library with metadata for UI display and testing.

Required coverage
- Diagram types: flowchart, sequence, class, state, ER, gantt, gitgraph, mindmap, journey, requirement, pie.
- Variants: compact, dense, long labels, nested groups, unicode, stress (large node/edge counts).

Metadata requirements
- Each sample includes: name, category, tags, complexity hint (S/M/L or node/edge count), and a 1-line description.
- Samples are deterministic and render without panics; failing samples must have an explicit error-expected tag.

Acceptance
- Sample library is large enough to show breadth (target 50-80 samples).
- UI can list/filter by category/tags without additional work.
","notes":"Expanded Mermaid sample library in mermaid_showcase.rs: added metadata fields (kind/complexity/features/edge_cases) and ~22 samples across flow/sequence/class/state/ER/gantt/mindmap/pie + unsupported gitgraph/journey/requirement. Updated docs/spec/mermaid-showcase.md catalog. Ran cargo fmt/check/clippy/fmt --check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:20:58.593696143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:51.575000165Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T14:56:54.388259453Z","close_reason":"Completed sample library + spec catalog","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","samples"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1lzl8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:34:51.574963245Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-28d7f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:50.496343614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2hpio","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:50.761155207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:20:58.593696143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2nkmi.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:21:01.168372402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2sp01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:49.973505859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3l6cd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:50.237364898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.3","title":"Implement screen state model + command handling","description":"What
- Implement the screen state model and command handling (single source of truth).

State requirements (minimum)
- samples list + selected_index
- layout_mode, fidelity tier, glyph_mode, wrap_mode, styles_enabled
- metrics_visible, controls_visible
- viewport_zoom + viewport_pan
- last_render report + last_error

Command handling
- Map keybindings to commands in one place (no scattered key logic).
- Commands deterministically update state and trigger re-render when appropriate.

Acceptance
- State transitions are deterministic and unit-testable.
- Re-render triggers are explicit (sample/layout/fidelity/style changes).

Quality gates: cargo fmt --check OK, cargo check --all-targets OK, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings OK.","created_at":"2026-02-05T14:39:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.4","title":"Integrate Mermaid render pipeline into new screen","description":"What
- Integrate the Mermaid pipeline end-to-end for the new screen.

Pipeline steps (must be explicit)
- Parse Mermaid source -> AST -> IR normalization.
- Layout: choose/compute layout based on mode and budgets.
- Render: draw into Buffer with viewport transforms.

Error handling
- Parse/layout/render errors must be surfaced in UI (status + diagnostics panel), not panic.
- If possible, show degraded/fallback output with reason.

Caching + invalidation
- Cache parsed IR/layout when inputs unchanged.
- Invalidate only when relevant state changes (sample, layout, fidelity, glyph/wrap modes, styles).

Acceptance
- Changing sample or layout recomputes correctly and updates metrics.
- Deterministic output for fixed state (snapshots stable).

Changes in mermaid_showcase.rs: (1) Added imports for mermaid, mermaid_layout, mermaid_render, Buffer, MermaidConfig, MermaidCompatibilityMatrix, MermaidFallbackPolicy (2) build_mermaid_config() builds config from state (tier, glyph mode, wrap mode, styles) (3) render_viewport() now runs parse -> normalize_ast_to_ir -> layout_diagram -> render_diagram_adaptive -> copy_from with error handling (error panel/overlay/source fallback) (4) Timing metrics collected via Cell<MermaidMetricsSnapshot> interior mutability (5) Footer/metrics panel show live timing data (6) Removed unused state.metrics field (7) 67 tests passing including 5 new render pipeline integration tests (all samples render at 80x24, 120x40, 40x12 without panics, metrics populated). Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check, cargo clippy all green for demo-showcase.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:12:57Z"},{"id":730,"issue_id":"bd-2nkmi.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LilacLynx: Pipeline integration code complete and tested (67 tests passing). Leaving in_progress until those are resolved.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:13:15Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","title":"Demo Input: central mouse dispatcher (hit grid + semantic events)","description":"Background
- The demo already enables hit testing (`Frame::enable_hit_testing`) and caches a hit grid for tab-bar navigation.
- Today, only the tab bar click path is routed (`handle_mouse_tab_click`). Many screens already register hit regions (panes, links, etc.) but they are not wired to input.

Goal
- Build a central, deterministic mouse dispatch layer in the demo app that routes mouse events to the right target:
  - Chrome (tabs, category tabs, status bar toggles).
  - Overlays (command palette, help overlay, tour overlay, modal stacks).
  - Active screen content.

Scope / Work Items
- Standardize the input pipeline:
  - Raw `Event::Mouse(MouseEvent)` -> (optional) semantic gestures -> dispatch.
  - Decide click activation edge: prefer `MouseUp` for buttons (prevents accidental drags) unless explicitly needed.
- Hit test on every relevant mouse event:
  - Use `(HitId, HitRegion, HitData)` from the cached hit grid.
  - Provide a consistent mapping from hit targets to actions.
- Add hover tracking:
  - Mouse move updates a `hover_hit` state (coalesced) to support hover highlight + status hints.
- Drag handling:
  - Minimal drag state machine for screens needing drag (kanban, drag-drop, sliders, timeline scrub).
  - Use existing `ftui-core` semantic event primitives if/when helpful.

Determinism / Performance Considerations
- Coalesce mouse move events aggressively (existing event coalescer already helps).
- Avoid per-frame allocations for routing; keep hit ids stable.
- Never introduce wall-clock time dependencies in gesture recognition unless time-freeze is part of determinism mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any registered hit region produces exactly one deterministic action.
- Hover state updates do not cause flicker and are coalesced.
- Drag interactions have a well-formed lifecycle and can be cancelled.
- Mouse interactions stop the guided tour consistently (like tab clicks already do).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for routing priority (palette > help > chrome > screen) and for hit mapping.
- Deterministic integration test: simulate a sequence of mouse events and assert resulting state transitions.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (hit grid cache + current mouse handling)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs` (HitId ranges)
- `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs` (click/drag semantics)
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid API)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.260604738Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","demo-showcase","hit-testing","input","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:39.942903936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.260566256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","title":"Widgets: standard hit regions + mouse semantics for core widgets","description":"Background
- The render layer supports hit testing via a per-cell hit grid.
- Many widgets already accept `hit_id(...)` and register hit regions in frames, but mouse semantics are not uniformly defined or exploited in the demo.

Goal
- Define and implement consistent mouse semantics for the core widget primitives used throughout the demo:
  - Lists, tables, trees, scrollbars, buttons/toggles, modals.

Scope / Work Items
- Hit region conventions:
  - Row/entry hit data encodes index (stable) so click can select/activate the right item.
  - Scrollbar hit regions include track/thumb and provide proportional scroll-to behavior.
  - Buttons/toggles use `HitRegion::Button` and activate on mouse up.
- Interaction semantics:
  - Click selects.
  - Double click activates/opens (where meaningful).
  - Scroll wheel scrolls the widget under hover/focus.
  - Drag thumb scroll works on scrollbars.
- Accessibility:
  - Hover and focus states are visible with high-contrast + large-text modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- The same mouse gestures behave the same across all screens using these widgets.
- Hit IDs and hit data are stable and deterministic across frames.

Tests / Validation
- Extend `ftui-widgets` frame integration tests for hit registration and hit data correctness.
- Add snapshot tests showing hover/selected states for at least one list and one table.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-render/src/frame.rs` (hit grid)
- `crates/ftui-widgets/*` (widgets)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui-widgets","hit-testing","mouse","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:40.702160542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","title":"Demo Chrome: tabs/palette/help/status overlays fully mouse interactive","description":"Background
- The demo chrome renders the tab bar, status bar, help overlay, and command palette.
- Tab bar hit regions exist and tab switching works, but most other chrome is keyboard-only.

Goal
- Make the demo's chrome and overlays fully mouse-clickable and scrollable in the obvious way.

Scope / Work Items
- Tab bar:
  - Hover highlight, click-to-switch (already exists), and optional scroll-wheel-to-cycle.
- Category tabs (if rendered):
  - Click category pill to jump.
- Command palette:
  - Hover highlight; click selects; double click executes; wheel scrolls list.
- Help overlay:
  - Clickable close button; wheel scroll if content overflows.
- Status bar:
  - Clickable affordances for the most common toggles (help, palette, a11y, perf HUD, debug) without ambiguity.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can navigate the entire demo UI with mouse alone (except text input entry).
- Mouse interactions are logged when demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for hit-id mapping for chrome elements.
- Snapshot tests for hover states + palette selection.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs`
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.544868719Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chrome","command-palette","demo-showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.544822323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","title":"Demo Shortcuts: global key standards + per-screen interactive keybindings","description":"Background
- The demo already supports a help overlay and per-screen keybinding lists.
- Many screens still feel passive; adding meaningful shortcuts is the easiest way to make the demo feel like a 'lab' rather than a gallery.

Goal
- Establish a consistent global shortcut vocabulary and expand per-screen shortcuts so each screen is discoverably interactive.

Global Shortcut Standards (proposed)
- `?` toggle help overlay.
- `Ctrl+K` command palette.
- `Ctrl+P` performance HUD.
- `Ctrl+I` inspector / hit regions overlay (if present).
- `M` toggle mouse mode (see mouse policy task).
- `Esc` closes the topmost overlay (palette/help/modal/tour).

Per-Screen Requirements
- Each screen must have at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts that change state or demonstrate a capability.
- Every shortcut must appear in help overlay under the active screen.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shortcut set is coherent across screens (same key means same kind of action).
- No conflicts with text input screens (respect focus).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for help overlay including global bindings + screen bindings.
- Snapshot tests for help overlay at 80x24 and 120x40 for at least 3 representative screens.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","help","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","title":"Guided Tour v2: add missing best-of moments + mouse/shortcut narration","description":"Background
- Guided tour mode exists and is deterministic, but the storyboard is currently not showcasing some of the most compelling screens and interactions.

Goal
- Expand and refine the guided tour to highlight the highest-signal demo features, with explicit narration of mouse/keyboard interactions.

Scope / Work Items
- Storyboard updates:
  - Ensure tour covers: Inline mode story, determinism lab, time-travel/snapshots, hit testing/hyperlinks, performance HUD/budgeting, and at least one 'big visual' (VFX/Quake).
  - Include at least 2 'interactive moments' where the tour explicitly demonstrates a click/drag/scroll or keypress sequence.
- Overlay copy updates:
  - Callouts should point out the exact interaction ('Click the tab', 'Scroll here', 'Press Ctrl+K').
  - Keep copy tight and high-signal.

Constraints
- Tour remains deterministic under fixed seed and time-freeze.
- Tour must not depend on real wall-clock delays.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tour completes in ~2-3 minutes and feels like a cinematic product tour.
- Tour demonstrates mouse and shortcuts explicitly.
- Tour does not break inline scrollback story.

Tests / Validation
- Update existing tour E2E harness to cover new steps and assert stable step ordering/log output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.512011698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.439885192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.203082627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.511911070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.979851049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.383701981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.677897754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.893529658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.286884579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.592109121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.144673588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.841142653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.477825496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.778447577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.073821838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","title":"Testing: mouse/shortcuts regression harness (unit + snapshot + PTY)","description":"Background
- Adding mouse routing and lots of shortcuts risks subtle regressions (overlay priority, focus bugs, scrollback theft, nondeterminism).

Goal
- Add a focused, deterministic test suite that locks in the new interaction behavior.

Scope / Work Items
- Unit tests (fast):
  - Mouse dispatch priority (palette > help > tour > chrome > screen).
  - Click activation edge consistency (down vs up).
  - Hover coalescing and drag lifecycle.
- Snapshot tests:
  - At least one snapshot per interaction category (palette hover, list click selection, scrollbar drag).
- PTY / E2E tests:
  - Send real SGR mouse sequences through PTY to validate parsing + routing.
  - Inline-mode run asserts scrollback-preserving defaults (mouse capture disabled unless toggled).

Acceptance Criteria
- Interaction behavior is deterministic and regression-protected.
- Tests are stable across sizes (80x24 and 120x40).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927421830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","e2e","mouse","shortcuts","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.746047285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.036084728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.333059545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.635703478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927366547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Tour screens","description":"Purpose
- Track and drive the mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for the demo's Tour-category experiences.

Included Screens (current)
- Guided Tour (base exists; v2 work tracked elsewhere in bd-iuvb.17.6)
- Dashboard
- Inline Mode Story (scrollback-first constraints)

Interaction Goals (Tour)
- Mouse-first navigation and 'clickable tiles' where the UI suggests it.
- Clear, cinematic affordances: hover highlight, click to drill in, wheel scroll where content overflows.
- Shortcuts that demonstrate capabilities quickly (toggle overlays, switch tiles, trigger scripted sequences).

Definition of Done
- Each included screen has at least 1 meaningful mouse interaction and at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts.
- Help overlay entries are accurate and tested.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
